title: Project 6 - Text Analysis
text: This project analyzes State of the Union Addresses from 1970 to 2021.

title: About
text:
My name is Erin!
I’m a senior studying Environmental Science and data science at UC Berkeley. I am ready to graduate :)
Why did I do this project?

Bc I want to get a good grade in Stat 133.

title: Project 6 - Text Analysis
text:
Analyses
This website presents two text analyses:

Analysis 1: Title of Your First Text Analysis
Analysis 2: Title of Your Second Text Analysis

title: Analysis 1
text:
Code
fsotu <- sotu |> slice(1) |> select(text)
ftokens <- unnest_tokens(tbl = fsotu, input = text, output = word)
ftokens <- anti_join(ftokens, stop_words, by = "word")

g1 <- ftokens |> 
  count(word, name = "count", sort = TRUE) |> 
  slice(1:20) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = count, y = reorder(word, count))) +
  labs(y = 'word') +
  geom_col()

ggplotly(g1)

title: Analysis 1
text:
Code
fsotu <- sotu |> slice(1) |> select(text)
ftokens <- unnest_tokens(tbl = fsotu, input = text, output = word)
ftokens <- anti_join(ftokens, stop_words, by = "word")

title: Analysis 1
section: What are the top 10 most common words in the latest address?
text:
What are the top 10 most common words in the latest address?


Code
lsotu <- sotu |> slice(n()) |> select(text)
ltokens <- unnest_tokens(tbl = lsotu, input = text, output = word)
ltokens <- anti_join(ltokens, stop_words, by = "word")

g2 <- ltokens |> 
  count(word, name = "count", sort = TRUE) |> 
  slice(1:20) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = count, y = reorder(word, count))) +
  labs(y = 'word') +
  geom_col()

ggplotly(g2)

title: Analysis 1
section: Top 10 words in each political party’s addresses
text:
Top 10 words in each political party’s addresses


Code
dem <- sotu |> filter(party == 'Democratic') |> select(text)
dtokens <- unnest_tokens(tbl = dem, input = text, output = word)
dtokens <- anti_join(dtokens, stop_words, by = "word")

g3 <- dtokens |> 
  count(word, name = "count", sort = TRUE) |> 
  slice(1:20) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = count, y = reorder(word, count))) +
  labs(y = 'word', title = 'Democrat') +
  geom_col()

ggplotly(g3)








Code
rep <- sotu |> filter(party == 'Republican') |> select(text)
rtokens <- unnest_tokens(tbl = rep, input = text, output = word)
rtokens <- anti_join(rtokens, stop_words, by = "word")

g4 <- rtokens |> 
  count(word, name = "count", sort = TRUE) |> 
  slice(1:20) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = count, y = reorder(word, count))) +
  labs(y = 'word', title = 'Republican') +
  geom_col()

ggplotly(g4)

title: Analysis 1
section: Top 10 most common words in all SOTU addresses
text:
Top 10 most common words in all SOTU addresses


Code
allsotu <- sotu |> select(text)
alltokens <- unnest_tokens(tbl = allsotu, input = text, output = word)
alltokens <- anti_join(alltokens, stop_words, by = "word")

g5 <- alltokens |> 
  count(word, name = "count", sort = TRUE) |> 
  slice(1:20) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = count, y = reorder(word, count))) +
  labs(y = 'word', title = 'All SOTU Addresses!') +
  geom_col()

ggplotly(g5)

title: Analysis 2
text:
Code
allsotu <- sotu |> select(text)
alltokens <- unnest_tokens(tbl = allsotu, input = text, output = word)
alltokens <- anti_join(alltokens, stop_words, by = "word")

allsent = inner_join(alltokens, sentiments, by = "word")


Warning in inner_join(alltokens, sentiments, by = "word"): Detected an unexpected many-to-many relationship between `x` and `y`.
ℹ Row 310579 of `x` matches multiple rows in `y`.
ℹ Row 6513 of `y` matches multiple rows in `x`.
ℹ If a many-to-many relationship is expected, set `relationship =
  "many-to-many"` to silence this warning.


Code
g6 <- allsent |> 
  count(word, sentiment, name = "count", sort = TRUE) |> 
  slice_head(n = 20) |> 
  ggplot(aes(x = count, y = reorder(word, count), fill = sentiment)) +
  geom_col()

ggplotly(g6)

title: Analysis 2
text:
First SOTU address contribution analysis


Code
fsotu <- sotu |> slice(1) |> select(text)
ftokens <- unnest_tokens(tbl = fsotu, input = text, output = word)
ftokens <- anti_join(ftokens, stop_words, by = "word")
fsent <- inner_join(ftokens, sentiments, by = "word")

g7 <- fsent |> 
  count(word, sentiment, name = "count", sort = TRUE) |> 
  slice_head(n = 20) |> 
  ggplot(aes(x = count, y = reorder(word, count), fill = sentiment)) +
  geom_col()

ggplotly(g7)

title: Analysis 2
text:
Latest SOTU address contribution analysis


Code
lsotu <- sotu |> slice(n()) |> select(text)
ltokens <- unnest_tokens(tbl = lsotu, input = text, output = word)
ltokens <- anti_join(ltokens, stop_words, by = "word")

lsent <- inner_join(ltokens, sentiments, by = "word")

g8 <- lsent |> 
  count(word, sentiment, name = "count", sort = TRUE) |> 
  slice_head(n = 20) |> 
  ggplot(aes(x = count, y = reorder(word, count), fill = sentiment)) +
  geom_col()

ggplotly(g8)

title: Analysis 2
text:
Sentiment dictionary comparison


Code
allafinn = inner_join(alltokens, afinn, by = "word")

g9 <- allafinn |> 
  count(word, value, name = "count", sort = TRUE) |> 
  slice_head(n = 20) |> 
  ggplot(aes(x = count, y = reorder(word, count), fill = value)) +
  labs(y = 'word', title = 'Afinn Lexicon') +
  geom_col()

ggplotly(g9)








Code
allnrc = inner_join(alltokens, nrc, by = "word")


Warning in inner_join(alltokens, nrc, by = "word"): Detected an unexpected many-to-many relationship between `x` and `y`.
ℹ Row 1 of `x` matches multiple rows in `y`.
ℹ Row 9888 of `y` matches multiple rows in `x`.
ℹ If a many-to-many relationship is expected, set `relationship =
  "many-to-many"` to silence this warning.


Code
g10 <- allnrc |> 
  count(word, sentiment, name = "count", sort = TRUE) |> 
  slice_head(n = 20) |> 
  ggplot(aes(x = count, y = reorder(word, count), fill = sentiment)) +
  labs(y = 'word', title = 'NRC Lexicon') +
  geom_col()

ggplotly(g10)






It’s definitely easier to interpret the afinn lexicon since things are on a numeric scale between good and bad. From the NRC graph though, we can see that there are a lot of “trusting” words. I wonder why congress is ‘disgust’ lol
